evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Setting standards for testing, documentation and CI/CD
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Testing, documentation, CI/CD, and data quality checks/monitoring recur across the responsibilities with no velocity/speed-of-shipping phrases present anywhere in the JD.
evidence.domain_risk: Our client is a well known consumer technology business with a platform used by millions of people.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: A consumer technology platform at scale where errors affect business decisions and user experience but carry no explicit regulatory, audit, or financial-reporting consequence, so moderate applies.
evidence.data_team_maturity: This is a new position and the first technical lead in the data engineering team. The team is small and learning dbt as they go
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The role is explicitly the first technical lead hire for a small team that is still learning dbt and migrating off legacy ETL, signalling the data engineering function is early in its formation despite the company's overall scale.
evidence.jd_authorship: You will own the dbt models and the semantic layer, decide how things get built, and coach the rest of the team, from experienced engineers through to analysts who have never touched dbt.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities describe a specific, concrete situation (new tech-lead role, team learning dbt, migration off legacy ETL) with named tools and a clear narrative of what a Tuesday looks like — precise and grounded, though it reads as a recruiter's paraphrase of the hiring manager's brief given the informal tone and "message me directly" sign-off.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: working closely with the analytics, data science and platform teams
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role serves internal data-function teams (analytics, data science, platform) directly, an internal_data orientation rather than a specific commercial, product, or finance audience.
evidence.autonomy_level: You will report to the Head of Data Engineering, who sets the overall direction. Your job is working out how to deliver it technically.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The JD explicitly separates strategic direction (owned by the Head of Data Engineering) from technical execution (owned by this role) — the candidate owns technical architecture decisions but not overall strategy, a genuine mixed signal.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD contains no mention of AI tools, AI-assisted coding, or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the text.
evidence.testing_framing: Setting standards for testing, documentation and CI/CD
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing standards are explicitly something the candidate sets and owns for the team, an ownership framing rather than a tool listed among requirements.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Building out sign and mend data quality checks and monitoring
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data quality monitoring is named as one of six responsibilities alongside team-building and migration work, a real but secondary concern rather than a dominant risk register, placing this at moderate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Leading the move away from legacy ETL onto dbt
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The dominant verb signal is migrating off an existing legacy ETL platform onto dbt and rebuilding the warehouse, a fix_scale framing even though some net-new semantic layer design is involved.
evidence.work_arrangement: On site: 2 days per week, London
evidence.loss_aversion: Building out sign and mend data quality checks and monitoring — quality/monitoring is named but is one responsibility among several team-building and migration priorities, not a dominant risk register.
